title: orobanche minor var. crithmi-maritimi
intro: Orobanche minor var. crithmi-maritimi, also known as Sea Mistletoe, is a parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by attaching itself to the roots of its host plant, usually Sea Thrift (Armeria maritima). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as an expectorant to relieve congestion. Orobanche minor var. crithmi-maritimi can be prepared as a herbal remedy in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its effectiveness and safety as a medicinal treatment have not been extensively scientifically studied.
